notes: Alcohol can be brought into Canada within duty-free limits subject to quantity and provincial rules
Some food items can be brought into Canada but strict agricultural restrictions apply
There is no limit on bringing cash into Canada, but amounts over CAD 10,000 must be declared.
Duty-free allowances apply when entering Canada, covering alcohol, tobacco, and other goods within set limits.
Personal electronics can be brought into Canada, but high-value items may be subject to duty.
Medication can be brought into Canada for personal use, but documentation and quantity limits may apply.
Tobacco can be brought into Canada within duty-free limits, but taxes apply above allowances.
Some weapons can be brought into Canada under strict regulations, but many are prohibited.
Prescription drugs can be brought into Canada for personal use if properly documented.
Travelers must declare certain goods when entering Canada, especially items exceeding limits or restricted goods.
